text: I feel curious yet slightly out of place. The repetition of gratitude in my conversation evokes a sense of polite awkwardness that lingers in the air. The transition from one environment—open, filled with people and polite exchanges—into another—a display of research and development—brings an intellectual stimulation, almost as if I myself am an exhibit in a gallery of evolving ideas. There is a detachment, yet also a subtle thrill at the edges, the anticipation of insight or unexpected connection. I appreciate the strangeness of this encounter: it feels both funny and subtly profound.
